perturbation.original_text: (2) In order for Proprietary Information disclosed by the Applicant to ARIN to be protected in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement, it must be: (a) in writing; (b) clearly identified as Proprietary Information at the time of its disclosure by each page thereof being marked with an appropriate legend, in bold faced print, indicating that the information is deemed proprietary by the Applicant; and (c) delivered by electronic mail, ARIN Online ticket, postal or courier service, or facsimile to the individual designated in Paragraph 3 below, or his designee. Where the Proprietary Information has not been or cannot be reduced to written form at the time of disclosure and such disclosure is made orally and with prior assertion of proprietary rights therein, such orally disclosed proprietary information shall only be protected in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement provided that complete written summaries of all proprietary aspects of any such oral disclosures shall have been delivered by Applicant to the individual identified in Paragraph 3 below, within 20 calendar days of said oral disclosures. The Applicant shall not identify information as proprietary which is not in good faith believed to be confidential, privileged, a trade secret, or otherwise entitled to such markings or proprietary claims.
perturbation.changed_text: (2) In order for Proprietary Information disclosed by the Applicant to ARIN to be protected in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement, it must be: (a) in writing; and (b) delivered by electronic mail, ARIN Online ticket, postal or courier service, or facsimile to the individual designated in Paragraph 3 below, or his designee. Where the Proprietary Information has not been or cannot be reduced to written form at the time of disclosure and such disclosure is made orally and with prior assertion of proprietary rights therein, such orally disclosed proprietary information shall only be protected in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement provided that complete written summaries of all proprietary aspects of any such oral disclosures shall have been delivered by Applicant to the individual identified in Paragraph 3 below, within 20 calendar days of said oral disclosures. The Applicant shall not identify information as proprietary which is not in good faith believed to be confidential, privileged, a trade secret, or otherwise entitled to such markings or proprietary claims.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement that proprietary information must be 'clearly identified as Proprietary Information' with a specific legend, it contradicts the intended spirit of the agreement. The agreement refers to identified proprietary information for protection, but the specific indication is removed, it creates a potential issue on what should be treated as such.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (4) ARIN covenants and agrees that it will take reasonable care to prevent the disclosure to any person or pe rsons outside its organization or to any unauthorized person or persons specifically identified by Applicant in writing, any and all Proprietary Information which is received from the Applicant under this Non-Disclosure Ag reement and which has been protected in accordance with paragraphs 2 and 3 hereof; provided, however, that ARIN shall not be liable for disclosure of such information if any or all of such information: A. Was in the public domain at the time it was disclosed, or B. Becomes part of the public domain without breach of this Agreement, or C. Is disclosed with the written approval of the Applicant, or D. Is disclosed after three years from ARIN's receipt of the information, or E. Was indepe ndently developed by ARIN, or F. Is or was disclosed by the Applicant to a third party without restriction, or G. Is disclosed pursuant to the provisions of a court order. As between the parties hereto, the provisions of this Paragraph 4 shall supersede the provisions of any inconsistent legend that may be affixed to said data by the Applicant, and the inconsistent provisions of any such legend shall be with out any force or effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires Proprietary Information to be 'clearly identified as Proprietary Information' to be protected. The changed text removes this requirement. The contradicted text states ARIN will prevent disclosure of 'Proprietary Information which has been protected in accordance with paragraphs 2 and 3'. Since paragraph 2 no longer requires clear identification, it creates a contradiction regarding which information ARIN is obligated to protect, creating a contradiction. The change alters a key requirement which breaks consistency, hence its a YES.

perturbation.original_text: (3) In order for the Applicant's Proprietary Information to be protected as described herein, the Proprietary Information must be delivered in written form as discussed in Paragraph 2 above to: American Registry for Internet Numbers Attn: Business Office PO Box 232290 Centreville, VA 20120 USA
perturbation.changed_text: (3) In order for the Applicant's Proprietary Information to be protected as described herein, the Proprietary Information must be delivered to: American Registry for Internet Numbers Attn: Business Office PO Box 232290 Centreville, VA 20120 USA
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase 'in written form as discussed in Paragraph 2 above' creates a contradiction. Paragraph 2 specifies requirements based on whether information is in written or oral form. By removing this reference, it is unclear whether the protections apply to all forms of proprietary information or only to information delivered in written form, therefore creating uncertainty, since it has previously described the protections and conditions for oral or written documentation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (2) In order for Proprietary Information disclosed by the Applicant to ARIN to be protected in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement, it must be: (a) in writing; and (b) delivered by electronic mail, ARIN Online ticket, postal or courier service, or facsimile to the individual designated in Paragraph 3 below, or his designee. Where the Proprietary Information has not been or cannot be reduced to written form at the time of disclosure and such disclosure is made orally and with prior assertion of proprietary rights therein, such orally disclosed proprietary information shall only be protected in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement provided that complete written summaries of all proprietary aspects of any such oral disclosures shall have been delivered by Applicant to the individual identified in Paragraph 3 below, within 20 calendar days of said oral disclosures. The Applicant shall not identify information as proprietary which is not in good faith believed to be confidential, privileged, a trade secret, or otherwise entitled to such markings or proprietary claims.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that proprietary information must be delivered 'in written form as discussed in Paragraph 2'. The changed text removes this condition. Paragraph 2 distinguishes between written and oral disclosures, outlining different protection requirements for each. By removing the 'in written form' requirement in Paragraph 3, the changed text creates a contradiction, because it is unclear whether the protection described in Paragraph 3 applies only to written information or to all forms, contradicting the original intent and conditions stipulated in Paragraph 2 regarding oral disclosures. This creates ambiguity and inconsistency, thus a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (4) ARIN covenants and agrees that it will take reasonable care to prevent the disclosure to any person or pe rsons outside its organization or to any unauthorized person or persons specifically identified by Applicant in writing, any and all Proprietary Information which is received from the Applicant under this Non-Disclosure Ag reement and which has been protected in accordance with paragraphs 2 and 3 hereof; provided, however, that ARIN shall not be liable for disclosure of such information if any or all of such information: A. Was in the public domain at the time it was disclosed, or B. Becomes part of the public domain without breach of this Agreement, or C. Is disclosed with the written approval of the Applicant, or D. Is disclosed after three years from ARIN's receipt of the information, or E. Was indepe ndently developed by ARIN, or F. Is or was disclosed by the Applicant to a third party without restriction, or G. Is disclosed pursuant to the provisions of a court order. As between the parties hereto, the provisions of this Paragraph 4 shall supersede the provisions of any inconsistent legend that may be affixed to said data by the Applicant, and the inconsistent provisions of any such legend shall be with out any force or effect.
perturbation.changed_text: (4) ARIN covenants and agrees that it will take reasonable care to prevent the disclosure to any person or pe rsons outside its organization or to any unauthorized person or persons specifically identified by Applicant in writing, any and all Proprietary Information which is received from the Applicant under this Non-Disclosure Ag reement; provided, however, that ARIN shall not be liable for disclosure of such information if any or all of such information: A. Was in the public domain at the time it was disclosed, or B. Becomes part of the public domain without breach of this Agreement, or C. Is disclosed with the written approval of the Applicant, or D. Is disclosed after three years from ARIN's receipt of the information, or E. Was indepe ndently developed by ARIN, or F. Is or was disclosed by the Applicant to a third party without restriction, or G. Is disclosed pursuant to the provisions of a court order. As between the parties hereto, the provisions of this Paragraph 4 shall supersede the provisions of any inconsistent legend that may be affixed to said data by the Applicant, and the inconsistent provisions of any such legend shall be with out any force or effect.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'and which has been protected in accordance with paragraphs 2 and 3 hereof' is removed, meaning there is no longer any qualification in section 4 that the proprietary information must be protected in line with paragraphs 2 and 3, before ARIN takes reasonable care to prevent disclosure. Now, it is less clear what actions the applicant must take to ensure ARIN covenants to protect the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (2) In order for Proprietary Information disclosed by the Applicant to ARIN to be protected in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement, it must be: (a) in writing; and (b) delivered by electronic mail, ARIN Online ticket, postal or courier service, or facsimile to the individual designated in Paragraph 3 below, or his designee. Where the Proprietary Information has not been or cannot be reduced to written form at the time of disclosure and such disclosure is made orally and with prior assertion of proprietary rights therein, such orally disclosed proprietary information shall only be protected in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement provided that complete written summaries of all proprietary aspects of any such oral disclosures shall have been delivered by Applicant to the individual identified in Paragraph 3 below, within 20 calendar days of said oral disclosures. The Applicant shall not identify information as proprietary which is not in good faith believed to be confidential, privileged, a trade secret, or otherwise entitled to such markings or proprietary claims.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section (4) states that ARIN will take reasonable care to prevent disclosure of proprietary information received from the applicant, 'and which has been protected in accordance with paragraphs 2 and 3 hereof'. This means the applicant has to protect the information according to paragraphs 2 and 3 for ARIN's covenant to apply. The changed text removes this qualification. Now, ARIN's obligation in section (4) is triggered regardless of whether the applicant has protected the information according to paragraphs 2 and 3. Paragraph (2), the contradicted text, specifies the actions the Applicant must take to ensure information is protected under the Non-Disclosure Agreement. By removing the phrase 'and which has been protected in accordance with paragraphs 2 and 3 hereof', the changed text creates a contradiction, because now ARIN's obligation exists regardless of whether the applicant complies with paragraph (2), which contradicts the intention and requirements of paragraph (2). This is an Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any Protected Information provided by the Applicant to ARIN shall be used only in furtherance of the purposes described in this Agreement, and shall be, upon written reque st at any time, returned to the Applicant. ARIN may discard or destroy any protected information three years after receiving it, provided Applicant has not requested ARIN to return the information before that time.
perturbation.changed_text: Any Protected Information provided by the Applicant to ARIN shall be used only in furtherance of the purposes described in this Agreement, and shall be, upon request at any time, returned to the Applicant. ARIN may discard or destroy any protected information three years after receiving it, provided Applicant has not requested ARIN to return the information before that time.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the word 'written' before the phrase 'request at any time' introduces a contradiction. The previous sections established requirements related to written communication, especially for disclosing information to be protected. By removing the 'written' qualifier for the request to return information, it opens the door for ambiguities about what constitutes a valid request (e.g., verbal, email, etc.) and potential disputes regarding whether a request was properly made.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (2) In order for Proprietary Information disclosed by the Applicant to ARIN to be protected in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement, it must be: (a) in writing; and (b) delivered by electronic mail, ARIN Online ticket, postal or courier service, or facsimile to the individual designated in Paragraph 3 below, or his designee. Where the Proprietary Information has not been or cannot be reduced to written form at the time of disclosure and such disclosure is made orally and with prior assertion of proprietary rights therein, such orally disclosed proprietary information shall only be protected in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement provided that complete written summaries of all proprietary aspects of any such oral disclosures shall have been delivered by Applicant to the individual identified in Paragraph 3 below, within 20 calendar days of said oral disclosures. The Applicant shall not identify information as proprietary which is not in good faith believed to be confidential, privileged, a trade secret, or otherwise entitled to such markings or proprietary claims.
perturbation.justification: The original text states '...upon written request...', which aligns with the contradicted text requiring that proprietary information disclosed by the applicant to ARIN must be in writing. The changed text removes the word 'written' before 'request', creating a contradiction because it now allows for non-written requests, which conflicts with the contradicted text's explicit requirement for written communication for protected information. The explanation provided states this removal introduces a contradiction, and by removing 'written' makes the return request ambiguous. Therefore, this qualifies as a strong in-text contradiction based on the definition of Omissions.

perturbation.original_text: (5) The standa rd of care for protecting Proprietary Information imposed on ARIN wi ll be th at degree of care ARIN uses to prevent disclosure, publication or dissemination of its own Proprietary Information.
perturbation.changed_text: (5) The standard of care for protecting Proprietary Information imposed on ARIN will be that of reasonable care.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the reference to ARIN's own Proprietary Information as the benchmark for the standard of care is an omission. Now that it only says 'reasonable care' there is a contradiction within the document, as reasonable care is not defined. The applicant can claim that reasonable care might not be enough, and ARIN might claim it is.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (4) ARIN covenants and agrees that it will take reasonable care to prevent the disclosure to any person or pe rsons outside its organization or to any unauthorized person or persons specifically identified by Applicant in writing, any and all Proprietary Information which is received from the Applicant under this Non-Disclosure Ag reement; provided, however, that ARIN shall not be liable for disclosure of such information if any or all of such information: A. Was in the public domain at the time it was disclosed, or B. Becomes part of the public domain without breach of this Agreement, or C. Is disclosed with the written approval of the Applicant, or D. Is disclosed after three years from ARIN's receipt of the information, or E. Was indepe ndently developed by ARIN, or F. Is or was disclosed by the Applicant to a third party without restriction, or G. Is disclosed pursuant to the provisions of a court order. As between the parties hereto, the provisions of this Paragraph 4 shall supersede the provisions of any inconsistent legend that may be affixed to said data by the Applicant, and the inconsistent provisions of any such legend shall be with out any force or effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined the standard of care as equivalent to how ARIN protects its own proprietary information. The changed text modifies this to 'reasonable care,' which creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, specifically paragraph 4, because reasonable care is subjective and undefined, making it difficult to determine what actions constitute compliance. Paragraph 4 also uses 'reasonable care' but doesn't define it either, which, according to the Perturbation Explanation, the applicant can claim that reasonable care might not be enough, and ARIN might claim it is. This introduces ambiguity and potential conflict, fitting the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.
